zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Rice於1583年8月18日在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特聖 馬丁 在 馮 菲爾茲受洗時，他的父親Henry Rice19歲，母親Margaret Baker21歲。他於1605年11月12日與Elizabeth Frost在英國素福爾可斯坦斯亞德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1621年11月16日在英國素福爾可斯坦斯亞德享年38歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦可拉肯維爾聖 加也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Rice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized pronunciation of one of the most common Welsh personal names, Rhys, from a form originally meaning ‘rash, impetuous’, also spelled Rys and Re(e)s. See also Reese , with which it is interchangeable as a result of different Anglicized forms of the Welsh vowel y, and also compare Preece and Price . Initial R- in Welsh is voiceless and often spelled Rh-, but in English R- is voiced as in the Anglicized surnames Rees and Rice. Welsh y is a short back vowel /ɪ/. In the medieval period the English approximation of this vowel was either /i/ or /e/, lengthened to /i:/ and /e:/. Subsequent sound changes in English produced the alternative pronunciations represented in Rees, Preece and Rice, Price. The name has also been established in Ireland from an early date.

English: either a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a thicket (Middle English ris, rice, ris, from Old English hrīs, Old Norse hrís), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place called with this word, such as Rise (East Yorkshire).

English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English Rys(e) and Re(e)s which when without a preposition could derive from one or other of several Old French and Middle English words, including Anglo-Norman French ris ‘laughter, smile’, Middle English ris, res ‘stem, stalk’, in origin the same word as in 2 above, and Middle English ris, rise, rice, res, Old French ris, riz ‘rice’, perhaps a nickname for a rice dealer or a cook.

variants.name: Reese
meanings: North German and Dutch (De Reese): nickname for a very big man, from Middle Low German, Middle Dutch rese ‘giant’.
German: habitational name from any of the places called Rees or Reese, in Rhineland and Lower Saxony.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: German: habitational name from a place in the Rhineland called Rees.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Andreas  .

variants.name: Ryce
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of   Rice  .

variants.name: Rise
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Aase, Lief, Sven.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of over twenty farmsteads named Rise, from Old Norse hrís ‘brushwood’. The name also occurs in Sweden and Denmark.

variants.name: Wrice
meanings: Probably Welsh or English: variant of   Rice  .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Rice was christened on 18 August 1583, in St Martin in the Fields, Westminster, Middlesex, England, his father, Henry Rice, was 19 and his mother, Margaret Baker, was 21. He married Elizabeth Frost on 12 November 1605, in Stanstead, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 16 November 1621, in Stanstead, Suffolk, England, at the age of 38, and was buried in St James' Church, Clerkenwell, London, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Riceが1583年8月18日にイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスタースト マルティン イン ス フィールズで洗礼を受けた時，父Henry Riceは19歳，母Margaret Bakerは21歳であった。1605年11月12日にイギリススフォルクスタンステアドでElizabeth Frostと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1621年11月16日にイギリススフォルクスタンステアドで38歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンクラーケンウエルスト ジャムス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Martin in the Fields, Westminster, Mi: 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 스트 마틴 인 테 필드즈
ko.St. James Churchyard, Clerkenwell, Londo: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 클레르켄웰 스트 자메스' 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Henry Rice(이)가 1583년 8월 18일 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 스트 마틴 인 테 필드즈에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Rice(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Baker(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1605년 11월 12일 영국 수폴크 스탠스테아드에서 Elizabeth Frost(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1621년 11월 16일 영국 수폴크 스탠스테아드에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 클레르켄웰 스트 자메스' 처르치에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Rice был крещен 18 августа 1583 г. в местности ст мартинь ин т фиелдс, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Rice было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Baker, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Frost 12 ноября 1605 г. в местности станстид, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 16 ноября 1621 г. в местности станстид, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 38 лет и был похоронен в местности ст джемес' чурч, клиркенвелл, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry Rice se bautizó el 18 de agosto de 1583, en St Martin in the Fields, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Rice, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Margaret Baker, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Frost el 12 de noviembre de 1605, en Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 16 de noviembre de 1621, en Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultado en St James' Church, Clerkenwell, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
